name: MIB-626 raised NAD and moved cardiometabolic measures but not physical function
summary: A short MIB-626 physiologic RCT strengthens NAD target-engagement coverage and adds limited cardiometabolic signals while explicitly limiting physical-function claims.
statement: In a 30-participant randomized 28-day study of overweight or obese middle-aged and older adults, MIB-626 raised circulating NAD and metabolites and lowered body weight, diastolic blood pressure, total cholesterol, LDL cholesterol, and non-HDL cholesterol versus placebo, but muscle strength, fatigability, aerobic capacity, stair-climbing power, insulin sensitivity, and hepatic or intra-abdominal fat did not differ significantly between groups.
population_or_model: Overweight or obese adults at least 45 years old
quantitative_note: Reported between-group differences included body weight -1.9 kg, diastolic blood pressure -7.01 mmHg, total cholesterol -26.89 mg/dL, LDL cholesterol -18.73 mg/dL, and no significant physical-performance differences.
caveats: The trial was small and short.
The positive signals are cardiometabolic physiology, not durable disease prevention or broad aging reversal.
The null physical-performance endpoints limit use of this source as broad human functional-benefit evidence.
Replication in larger independent trials is needed.
